subject: [PATCH v10 14/15] selftests/mm: add userfaultfd RWP tests

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the selftests build is failing due to an undeclared identifier UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP, suggesting that tools/include/uapi/linux/userfaultfd.h needs updates from include/uapi/linux/userfaultfd.h.
reviews.raw_body:
I'm getting selftests build errors:

uffd-unit-tests.c: In function \u2018uffd_register_rwp\u2019:
uffd-unit-tests.c:624:25: error: \u2018UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP\u2019 undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean \u2018UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_WP\u2019?
  624 |                 .mode = UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP,
      |                         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                         UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_WP
uffd-unit-tests.c:624:25: note: each undeclared identifier is reported only once

<etc>

Seems that tools/include/uapi/linux/userfaultfd.h needs updates from
include/uapi/linux/userfaultfd.h?

I'll disable this patch for now.

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch adds userfaultfd read-write protect (RWP) tests to the Linux kernel's selftest suite. The tests cover various scenarios, including asynchronous and synchronous RWP modes, page mapping, mprotect, GUP walks, and fork operations. The tests verify that RWP works correctly in different memory types, such as anonymous, shared memory, and huge pages. The patch also includes a test to ensure that registering with both write-protect and read-write protect modes returns an error.

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mike Rapoport questioned the necessity of including <linux/fs.h> and <sys/uio.h> in the uffd-unit-tests.c file, suggesting that they are already brought into selftests by make install_headers.
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm, I wonder if it should be there at all :/

I thought userfaultfd.h is brought into selftests by make install_headers

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the RWP snapshot was dropped on purpose and promised to correct it by folding the RWP delta into tools/include/uapi/linux/userfaultfd.h, matching a previous commit's mechanism.

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch adds userfaultfd read-write protect (RWP) tests to the Linux kernel's selftest suite. The tests cover various scenarios, including asynchronous and synchronous RWP modes, page mapping, mprotect, GUP walks, and fork operations. The tests verify that RWP works correctly in different memory types, such as anonymous, shared memory, and huge pages. The patch also includes a test to ensure that registering with both write-protect and read-write protect modes returns an error.
